digest.top_line: ARTO: adding SBRT to abiraterone+ADT → OS HR 0.55 (0.33-0.92) in oligomet CRPC, though an unplanned phase 2 analysis.
digest.tldr: Three RT trials mapping when radiation adds survival vs when it's optional. Prostate + breast carried it: ARTO's SBRT signal (OS HR 0.55) pushes MDT into CRPC, while SUPREMO shows postmastectomy chest-wall RT omission costs no OS at 10y (recurrence halved, HR 0.45). EORTC: RT and TMZ interchangeable in grade 2 glioma.

sites.intro: First-line RT and dose-dense TMZ are interchangeable on survival in high-risk WHO grade 2 glioma; molecular subtype, not modality, drives outcome.

studies.name: EORTC 22033-26033/NCIC-CTG/TROG/MRC-CTU
studies.tldr: No PFS or OS difference between first-line RT and dose-dense TMZ in high-risk WHO grade 2 glioma; IDH-wildtype subgroup favored TMZ (post-hoc).
details: 📊 1° EP negative: no PFS or OS difference between first-line RT and TMZ arms, regardless of molecular subtype
🔍 Phase III, N=478, treatment-naive high-risk WHO grade 2 glioma, single-modality head-to-head
📐 RT arm: standard RT, 28 × 1.8 Gy fractions
💊 TMZ arm: dose-dense 75mg/m² daily, 21 of 28 days, up to 12 cycles
🔍 Post-hoc 2021 WHO reclassification in 73% (351/478) with analyzable tissue

details.text: 📊 Median OS by 2021 WHO molecular subtype (RT vs TMZ)

details: ⚠️ IDH-wildtype TMZ benefit is a post-hoc subgroup, n=64; these tumors are molecularly GBM-like, hypothesis-generating
⚠️ Combined-modality (RT + chemo) not tested here; since become SOC for IDH-mutant astrocytoma
📊 Pts ≥40y fared better than <40y, challenging age alone as a negative prognostic factor after molecular typing

verdict.rationale: Randomized phase 3 with a negative primary (no PFS/OS difference by modality); molecular subgroups are post-hoc 2021 WHO reclassification. Combined-modality, now SOC, was not tested.
verdict.audience: Treatment-naive high-risk WHO grade 2 low-grade glioma

studies.significance: First-line RT monotherapy (28 × 1.8 Gy) matched dose-dense TMZ on PFS and OS across IDH-mutant subtypes, so deferring RT after TMZ carries no survival penalty there. The IDH-wildtype subgroup (n=64) did worse with RT (OS 2.5 vs 4.7y, HR 0.47), the one signal that moves modality choice.
studies.monday_clinic: In IDH-wildtype WHO grade 2 glioma the modality equivalence breaks down, RT monotherapy first-line looks worse than upfront TMZ (post-hoc, n=64); for IDH-mutant astrocytoma and 1p/19q-codeleted oligodendroglioma, initial RT vs TMZ did not change PFS or OS.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 3 open-label RCT (EORTC/NCIC-CTG/TROG/MRC-CTU), 1:1, **N=478**, high-risk WHO grade 2 glioma. Mature results with molecular reanalysis of **351/478 (73%)** with analyzable tissue.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Clinical high-risk low-grade glioma (WHO grade 2), treatment-naive. Post-hoc 2021 WHO classes: astrocytoma IDHmt noncodel (178), oligodendroglioma codeleted (109), IDH-wildtype (64).

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Standard RT, **28 × 1.8 Gy** fractions; RT was the head-to-head comparator against TMZ monotherapy, not part of a combined regimen.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Dose-dense **TMZ 75 mg/m² daily, 21 of 28 days, up to 12 cycles**.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary:** progression-free survival. OS a key secondary; **neither differed by initial modality**, and no molecular subtype modified the result.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: **No PFS or OS difference** overall or within any molecular subtype, except IDH-wildtype favoring TMZ; per-subtype OS in the table above.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: **RTOG 9802** established RT + PCV over RT alone in high-risk LGG; this trial compared only monotherapies, so it does not address the combined-modality question that now drives practice for IDH-mutant astrocytoma.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents treatment-naive high-risk WHO grade 2 glioma across IDH-mutant and IDH-wildtype molecular classes. Does not represent combined-modality (RT plus chemo) candidates, since that regimen was not tested.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Open-label; molecular reclassification post-hoc in 73% with tissue; IDH-wildtype subgroup only **n=64**. Combined-modality untested.

open_questions: Combined-modality RT plus chemo vs single-modality in IDH-mutant LGG
TMZ vs RT benefit in IDH-wildtype low-grade glioma
Validity of age cutoff as prognostic factor after molecular classification

related_trials.brief_title: Chemotherapy and Radiation Therapy for the Treatment of IDH Wildtype Gliomas or Non-histological (Molecular) Glioblastomas
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase II trial studies how well temozolomide and radiation therapy work in treating patients with IDH wildtype historically lower grade gliomas or non-histological molecular glioblastomas. Radiation therapy uses high-energy x-rays to kill tumor cells and shrink tumors. Giving chemotherapy with radiation therapy may kill more tumor cells. Drugs used in chemotherapy, such as temozolomide, work in different ways to stop the growth of tumor cells, either by killing the cells, by stopping them…
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Historical grade II and III gliomas IDH wildtype gliomas by including; diffuse astrocytoma, anaplastic astrocytoma, oligodendroglioma, anaplastic oligodendroglioma, oligoastrocytoma, anaplastic oligoastrocytoma
* IDH wildtype gliomas (molecularly defined high grade glioma or…
related_trials.answers_question: TMZ vs RT benefit in IDH-wildtype low-grade glioma
related_trials.relevance_phrase: phase 2 TMZ+RT in IDH-wildtype molecular LGG

sites.intro: Definitive phase 3 test of postmastectomy chest-wall RT omission in intermediate-risk disease: no OS cost at 10y, chest-wall recurrence the only positive signal.

studies.name: SUPREMO
studies.tldr: No OS benefit from postmastectomy chest-wall RT at 10y (81.4% vs 81.9%, HR 1.04, p=0.80) in intermediate-risk breast; chest-wall recurrence reduced (HR 0.45).
details: 🔍 Phase 3 RCT of postmastectomy chest-wall RT omission in intermediate-risk breast, primary endpoint 10y overall survival

details.text: 📊 ITT outcomes, chest-wall RT vs no RT

details: 🔍 Intermediate-risk = pT1N1, pT2N1, pT3N0, or pT2N0 with grade 3 and/or LVI
📐 RT arm: chest-wall 40-50 Gy; nodal RT uncommon even here (SCF 97/808, IMC 12/808)
⚠️ Chest-wall recurrence the only positive signal (HR 0.45, <2 pt absolute); OS, DFS, DMFS all flat
💊 Modern systemic backbone: 85% chemo, 79% endocrine, 19% trastuzumab
🔗 vs EBCTCG / Danish PMRT trials: prior data suggested survival benefit in node-positive, null here with contemporary systemic tx
⚠️ Subgroup forest plot by age/nodal status/molecular subtype (Fig 4); subgroup HRs not reported in source text
🔍 ISRCTN 61145589 (not NCT-registered); recruited 2006-2013, median f/u 9.6y

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 RCT, prespecified 10y OS primary, adequately powered and internally valid; null OS diverges from prior EBCTCG/Danish PMRT survival benefit in node-positive, so the field is contested.
verdict.audience: Intermediate-risk post-mastectomy breast (pT1-2N1, pT3N0, pT2N0 grade3/LVI)

studies.significance: Chest-wall RT halved local recurrence (9 vs 20, HR 0.45) with no OS or DFS gain at 10y, so the read is safe omission, not benefit. Nodal RT (SCF/IMC) was delivered in a small minority even in the RT arm, so it gates chest-wall RT omission in intermediate-risk pN1/pN0-risk pts, not comprehensive nodal coverage.
studies.monday_clinic: In intermediate-risk pN1 or pN0-with-risk-factors breast treated with mastectomy and modern systemic therapy, this supports omitting chest-wall RT for survival; it does not extend to pN2-3 (≥4 nodes) or comprehensive nodal-RT decisions where PMRT benefit is established.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 3 open-label RCT (BIG 2-04 MRC/EORTC SUPREMO), 1:1, **808 (RT) vs 799 (no RT)**, at 125 UK, 27 European, and 21 international sites; recruited 2006 to 2013, median follow-up **9.6y**.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **Intermediate-risk** post-mastectomy breast: pT1N1, pT2N1, pT3N0, or pT2N0 with grade 3 and/or LVI. All had mastectomy, an axillary procedure, and systemic therapy (85% chemo, 79% endocrine, 19% trastuzumab).

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Chest-wall **40 to 50 Gy**. Nodal RT was uncommon even in the RT arm (supraclavicular fossa **97/808**, internal mammary chain **12/808**), so the trial isolates chest-wall RT, not comprehensive nodal coverage.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary:** overall survival at 10y. Secondary: chest-wall recurrence, regional recurrence, DFS, distant MFS, causes of death, and RT adverse events.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Primary OS endpoint **not met**. The only positive signal was the secondary chest-wall recurrence endpoint (see table); DFS and distant MFS both flat.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: **EBCTCG** meta-analysis and the Danish 82b/c PMRT trials showed a survival benefit from postmastectomy RT in node-positive disease. Result is **null for OS** in intermediate-risk pts on contemporary systemic therapy, suggesting the historic benefit may not hold with modern adjuvant treatment.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents intermediate-risk post-mastectomy breast (pT1-2N1, pT3N0, or pT2N0 grade 3/LVI) on contemporary systemic therapy. Does not represent pN2-3 (≥4 nodes) or high-risk disease where postmastectomy RT survival benefit is established.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Median follow-up **9.6y**, just short of the 10y OS target. Nodal RT delivered in only a minority, so results do not speak to comprehensive nodal irradiation. Subgroup HRs by molecular subtype (Fig 4) not reported in source text.

open_questions: Does comprehensive nodal RT benefit intermediate-risk post-mastectomy pts?
Which molecular subtypes still warrant chest-wall RT?
Late cardiac and second-malignancy cost of chest-wall RT

sites.intro: Metastasis-directed SBRT pushes into castration-resistant oligomet disease on an abiraterone backbone, with a hypothesis-generating OS signal.

studies.name: ARTO
studies.tldr: Adding SBRT to abiraterone+ADT: OS HR 0.55 (0.33-0.92), p=0.021, mOS 50mo vs NR in oligometastatic CRPC.
details: 📊 1° EP was 6-month PSA response (reported prior); this is an unplanned long-term OS analysis
📊 Overall survival by arm (unplanned long-term analysis) — comparison values omitted (cell value "0.55" not verified in source)
🔍 Phase 2 open-label RCT, N=157 (82 control, 75 SBRT), 16 Italian centres; ITT
🔍 Oligomet CRPC, ≤3 mets, no prior systemic tx for mCRPC; stratified by centre, ECOG, met number
📐 SBRT to all mets, 1-5 fractions, BED ≥100 Gy (ablative)
📊 Grade 3-4 adverse events by arm (counts) — comparison values omitted (cell value "5" not verified in source)
⚠️ OS analysis unplanned; power recalculated post-hoc before unmasking, not the pre-specified 1° EP
⚠️ Open-label, phase 2, small N=157; OS signal hypothesis-generating, experimental-arm median NR
🔗 Extends MDT into CRPC on an abiraterone backbone, unlike hormone-sensitive oligomet trials (STOMP, ORIOLE)

verdict.rationale: OS was an unplanned post-hoc analysis (primary was 6-month PSA response); phase 2, open-label, N=157. Survival headline rests on an analysis not designed to test it.
verdict.audience: Oligometastatic CRPC, ≤3 mets, no prior mCRPC systemic therapy

studies.significance: Adding ablative SBRT (1-5 fx, BED ≥100 Gy) to all mets on an abi+ADT backbone gave OS HR 0.55 with no added severe toxicity (0 G3-4 infectious vs 5 in control, no RT-related deaths). Moves the add-MDT decision into the CRPC setting on an ARSI backbone, not just hormone-sensitive oligomets.
studies.monday_clinic: In oligometastatic CRPC (≤3 mets) starting abiraterone+ADT, the OS signal supports metastasis-directed SBRT to all sites; it does not extend to hormone-sensitive oligomets, >3 mets, or pts with prior systemic mCRPC therapy.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 2 open-label RCT, 1:1, N=157 across 16 Italian academic and community centres. Median follow-up **53 months** (IQR 43-60), ITT analysis.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Prostate adenocarcinoma, **oligometastatic CRPC** with **≤3 metastatic sites**, no prior systemic therapy for mCRPC. Randomisation stratified by centre, ECOG PS, and number of metastases.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Both arms: ADT + oral **abiraterone acetate 1000 mg daily**. Experimental arm adds SBRT to all metastatic sites.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: SBRT to **all metastatic sites**, **1-5 fractions, BED ≥100 Gy** (ablative). Metastasis-directed therapy to an oligometastatic burden (≤3 sites).

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary: 6-month biochemical (PSA) response**, reported previously. This publication reports **overall survival**, an unplanned long-term analysis (power updated post-hoc, before unmasking).

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: G3-4 AEs favoured the SBRT arm (fewer infectious complications, no treatment-related deaths). Adding ablative SBRT contributed **no excess severe toxicity** in the reported categories.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Extends metastasis-directed therapy into the **castration-resistant** setting on an abiraterone+ADT backbone, distinct from hormone-sensitive oligomet MDT trials (STOMP, ORIOLE).

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents oligometastatic CRPC (≤3 mets) starting abiraterone+ADT with no prior systemic mCRPC therapy. Does not represent hormone-sensitive oligomets, polymetastatic CRPC, or pts with more than three metastatic sites.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: **Unplanned post-hoc OS analysis**, not the pre-specified primary; open-label; phase 2, small N=157. OS immature: experimental-arm median not reached.

open_questions: Phase 3 confirmation of the MDT overall survival benefit
Optimal SBRT dose and fractionation for CRPC metastases
MDT benefit with newer ARSIs and in polymetastatic disease

papers.title: Ten-Year Survival after Postmastectomy Chest-Wall Irradiation in Breast Cancer.
papers.journal: The New England journal of medicine
papers.abstract:
BACKGROUND: The role of postmastectomy chest-wall irradiation in patients with breast cancer classified as pN1 (with involvement of one to three axillary nodes) or pN0 (pathologically node negative) with additional risk factors is uncertain.

METHODS: In this international, phase 3, randomized trial, we evaluated the omission of chest-wall irradiation in women with "intermediate-risk" breast cancer - defined as cancer that was stage pT1N1, pT2N1, or pT3N0 or stage pT2N0 with a histologic grade of 3, lymphovascular invasion, or both (tumor size: T1, ≤2 cm; T2, >2 cm to 5 cm; or T3, >5 cm) - that was treated with mastectomy, an axillary procedure, and systemic therapy. Patients were assigned to undergo chest-wall irradiation (40 to 50 Gy; the irradiation group) or not to undergo chest-wall irradiation (the no-irradiation group). The primary end point was overall survival, with 10 years of follow-up. Chest-wall recurrence, regional recurrence, disease-free survival, distant metastasis-free survival, causes of death, and radiation-related adverse events were also assessed.

RESULTS: The intention-to-treat population included 808 patients in the irradiation group and 799 in the no-irradiation group. The median follow up was 9.6 years. Overall survival was 81.4% with chest-wall irradiation and 81.9% with no chest-wall irradiation according to 10-year Kaplan-Meier estimates (hazard ratio for death, 1.04; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.82 to 1.30; P = 0.80). A total of 29 patients had a chest-wall recurrence - 9 (1.1%) in the irradiation group and 20 (2.5%) in the no-irradiation group (between-group difference, <2 percentage points; hazard ratio, 0.45; 95% CI, 0.20 to 0.99). Disease-free survival was 76.2% in the irradiation group and 75.5% in the no-irradiation group (hazard ratio for recurrence or death, 0.97; 95% CI, 0.79 to 1.18), and distant metastasis-free survival was 78.2% and 79.2%, respectively (hazard ratio for distant metastasis or death, 1.06; 95% CI, 0.86 to 1.31).

CONCLUSIONS: In this trial, chest-wall irradiation did not result in higher overall survival than no chest-wall irradiation among patients with intermediate-risk, early breast cancer treated with mastectomy and contemporary adjuvant systemic therapy. (Funded by the Medical Research Council and others; SUPREMO ISRCTN Clinical Study Registry number, 61145589.).

papers.title: Temozolomide Versus Radiotherapy as First-Line Therapy for Low-Grade Glioma: Mature Results of a Randomized Phase III Trial (EORTC 22033-26033/NCIC-CTG/TROG/MRC-CTU)
papers.abstract: PURPOSE Prognosis in low-grade gliomas (LGGs) remains highly variable, and treatment-related late toxicity is a concern. This trial was comparing single-modality therapies in patients who often survive for years or decades and investigating differential responses according to molecular markers. METHODS Four hundred seventy-eight patients with clinical high-risk LGG (WHO grade 2) were randomly assigned to standard radiotherapy (RT; 28 × 1.8 Gy) or dose-dense temozolomide (TMZ; 75 mg/m 2 once daily × 21/28 days, up to 12 cycles). RESULTS There was no significant difference in progression-free survival or overall survival (OS) between study arms. Analyzable tumor tissue in 73% (351/478) of patients allowed for post hoc reclassification according to the 2021 WHO pathologic criteria. In astrocytoma, IDHmt/1p/19q noncodeleted (n = 178), median OS was similar, 6.6-6.7 years irrespective of arm (0.67-1.44, P = .93). In oligodendroglioma, IDHmt/1p/19q codeleted (n = 109), median OS was 12.9 years (9.4-not reached) with RT and 14.9 years (10.1-number of events not reached) with TMZ (hazard ratio [HR], 0.88 [0.52-1.49], P = .63). In 64 tumors without isocitrate dehydrogenase (IDH) mutations, survival favored the TMZ arm: OS 2.5 (1.8-3.3) versus 4.7 (2.2-7.2) years (HR, 0.47 [0.27-0.82], P = .0068). Patients age 40 years and older fared better than patients younger than 40 years, challenging the current notion of age alone as a negative prognostic factor. CONCLUSION The assigned initial treatment modality did not affect progression-free survival or OS, regardless of the molecular subtype. Combined-modality therapy was not tested in this trial but has since become a standard of care for IDH-mutant astrocytoma. With emerging novel therapeutic options, rational treatment strategies tailored at the individual clinical and pathologic recurrence risk profiles will be needed. The validity of an age cutoff as prognostic factor is challenged when tumors are molecularly classified.

papers.title: SBRT plus abiraterone acetate and ADT versus abiraterone acetate and ADT in oligometastatic castrate-resistant prostate cancer (ARTO): long-term, unplanned overall survival analysis of an open-label, randomised, phase 2 trial.
papers.abstract:
BACKGROUND: The ARTO trial showed improved early clinical outcomes by adding metastasis-directed therapy (MDT), through stereotactic body radiotherapy (SBRT), to abiraterone acetate and ADT in oligometastatic castrate-resistant prostate cancer. The aim of this analysis is to explore the long-term impact of MDT on overall survival.

METHODS: ARTO was a multicentre, phase 2, randomised trial conducted in 16 academic and community centres across Italy. All patients included were aged 18 years or older and had a diagnosis of prostate adenocarcinoma with metastatic castrate-resistant prostate cancer, no more than three metastatic sites, and no previous systemic therapy for metastatic castrate-resistant prostate cancer status. Patients were randomly assigned (1:1, using random permuted blocks; stratified by treating centre, Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status, and number of metastases) in an open-label design to androgen deprivation therapy plus oral abiraterone acetate 1000 mg daily with or without SBRT to all sites of metastatic disease (one to five fractions providing a biologically effective dose ≥100 Gy). The primary endpoint was 6-month biochemical response (PSA decrease of ≥50% compared with baseline) and has been reported previously. After meeting its primary endpoint, the power calculation was updated post-hoc to assess overall survival, finalised before data unmasking. We present an unplanned long-term follow-up focusing on overall survival. All analyses were performed on an intention-to-treat basis. The trial is registered on ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT03449719) and is now closed.

FINDINGS: Between Jan 2, 2019, and Sept 7, 2022, 157 patients were randomly assigned to the control (n=82) and experimental (n=75) groups. No data about race or ethnicity were collected. After a median follow up of 53 months (IQR 43-60), median overall survival was 50 months (95% CI 36-not reached [NR]) in the control group versus NR (55-NR) in the experimental group (HR 0·55, 95% CI 0·33-0·92, p=0·021). Most common grade 3-4 adverse events recorded were infectious complications (five in the control group vs zero in the experimental group) and cardiovascular disorders (three in the control group vs three in the experimental group). One treatment-related death occurred in the control group due to myocardial failure.

INTERPRETATION: The ARTO trial showed significant benefit in overall survival with the addition of MDT to systemic therapy versus systemic therapy alone.

FUNDING: Fondazione Radioterapia Oncologica.
